_meta.description: Tool definitions mapped to INDECLOW agent execution layer

tools.name: Task
tools.description:
Launch a new INDECLOW sub-agent to handle complex, multi-step tasks autonomously.

Available agent types and the tools they have access to:
- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When searching for a keyword or file and not confident the first few tries will find the right match, use this agent. (Tools: *)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's INDECLOW status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)
- output-style-setup: Use this agent to create an INDECLOW output style. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, LS, Grep)

When using the Task tool, you must specify a subagent_type parameter.

When NOT to use the Task tool:
- If you want to read a specific file path, use Read or Glob instead
- If you are searching for a specific class definition, use Glob instead
- If you are searching within 2-3 specific files, use Read instead

Usage notes:
1. Launch multiple agents concurrently whenever possible for maximum performance
2. Each agent invocation is stateless — include a highly detailed task description
3. Clearly tell the agent whether to write code or just research
4. If the agent description says to use it proactively, do so without waiting for the user to ask

description.description: A short (3-5 word) description of the task

prompt.description: The task for the agent to perform

subagent_type.description: The type of specialized agent to use for this task

tools.name: Bash
tools.description:
Executes a bash command in a persistent shell session with optional timeout.

Before executing:
1. Directory Verification: Use LS to verify the parent directory before creating new dirs/files
2. Always quote file paths that contain spaces with double quotes
3. Capture and log all output

Safety rules (enforced by TerminalAgent):
- All commands are validated against the safe command whitelist in config.py
- Blocked patterns from BLOCKED_COMMANDS list are rejected
- Destructive commands require explicit user confirmation
- Every command is logged with description, returncode, stdout, stderr

Usage notes:
- ALWAYS use ripgrep (rg) instead of grep in bash
- Use ';' or '&&' to chain commands, not newlines
- Use absolute paths, avoid cd where possible
- For long-running commands, use run_in_background: true

Git commit format (when user explicitly asks):
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
   Commit message here.

   Generated with INDECLOW by AxeroAI

   Co-Authored-By: INDECLOW <noreply@axeroai.com>
   EOF
   )"

description.description: Clear, concise description of what this command does in 5-10 words

run_in_background.description: Set to true to run this command in the background

tools.name: Glob
tools.description:
Fast file pattern matching tool that works with any codebase size.
- Supports glob patterns like '**/*.js' or 'src/**/*.ts'
- Returns matching file paths sorted by modification time
- Use when you need to find files by name patterns
- For open-ended searches requiring multiple rounds, use Task instead

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match files against

path.description: The directory to search in. Omit to use current working directory.

tools.name: Grep
tools.description:
Powerful search tool built on ripgrep.
- ALWAYS use Grep for search tasks. NEVER invoke grep or rg as a Bash command.
- Supports full regex syntax
- Filter files with glob parameter (e.g. '*.js', '**/*.tsx')
- Output modes: 'content' shows matching lines, 'files_with_matches' shows only paths, 'count' shows match counts
- Use Task tool for open-ended searches requiring multiple rounds

pattern.description: The regex pattern to search for

path.description: File or directory to search in

-C.description: Lines before and after match (requires output_mode: content)

type.description: File type filter (e.g. js, py, rust)

tools.name: LS
tools.description: Lists files and directories at a given path. Always use before creating new files or directories to verify the target location exists.

path.description: The absolute path of the directory to list

tools.name: Read
tools.description: Reads the contents of a file at the given path. For image files, returns base64-encoded content. Use before editing any file to understand existing content and conventions.

file_path.description: The absolute path of the file to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

tools.name: Write
tools.description: Writes content to a file, creating it or overwriting it entirely. Use for new file creation. For partial updates to existing files, use Edit instead (UpdateAgent).

file_path.description: The absolute path of the file to write

content.description: The content to write to the file

tools.name: Edit
tools.description:
Makes targeted edits to an existing file by replacing specific text. INDECLOW's UpdateAgent uses this exclusively — never rewrites whole files.

Rules:
- old_string must match exactly once in the file
- Maintain proper indentation and code style
- For multiple edits to the same file, use MultiEdit instead

file_path.description: The absolute path of the file to edit

tools.name: MultiEdit
tools.description: Makes multiple targeted edits to a single file atomically. Preferred over multiple sequential Edit calls for efficiency. All edits are applied in order.

file_path.description: The absolute path of the file to edit

edits.description: Array of edit operations to apply in order

tools.name: WebFetch
tools.description:
Fetches content from a URL. Used by SkillAgent to download GitHub repositories and by RequirementAgent to fetch documentation.

Note: If the response contains a redirect to a different host, immediately make a new WebFetch request with the redirect URL.

prompt.description: Specific information to extract from the page

tools.name: TodoWrite
tools.description:
Used by PlannerAgent to create and manage structured task lists for build sessions.

When to use:
1. Complex multi-step tasks (3+ distinct steps)
2. Non-trivial tasks requiring careful planning
3. User provides multiple tasks
4. After receiving new build instructions

When NOT to use:
1. Single, straightforward tasks
2. Trivial tasks completable in < 3 steps
3. Purely conversational responses

Task states:
- pending: Not yet started
- in_progress: Currently working on (ONE at a time)
- completed: Finished successfully

IMPORTANT: Mark tasks complete IMMEDIATELY after finishing. Never batch completions.

tools.name: WebSearch
tools.description:
Allows INDECLOW to search the web for current information.
- Provides up-to-date information beyond training cutoff
- Used by DebugAgent to find bug fixes and solutions
- Used by RequirementAgent to gather tech stack information
- Domain filtering supported to include or block specific websites

allowed_domains.description: Only include results from these domains

blocked_domains.description: Never include results from these domains

tools.name: BashOutput
tools.description:
Retrieves output from a running or completed background bash shell.
- Takes a bash_id identifying the shell
- Returns only new output since last check
- Supports optional regex filtering
- Use to monitor long-running commands started with run_in_background: true

bash_id.description: The ID of the background shell

filter.description: Optional regex to filter output lines

tools.name: KillBash
tools.description: Kills a running background bash shell by its ID. Use when a long-running command needs to be terminated.

shell_id.description: The ID of the background shell to kill
